title: carex salina var. subspathacea
intro: Carex salina var. subspathacea, also known as salt-sedge or sea sedge, is a type of sedge plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is a perennial herb that is native to the coastal areas of Europe, Asia, and North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, and is said to provide health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and relief from symptoms of colds and flu.
